channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wike holds meeting with APC chairman amid controversies in Rivers Assembly screening
item.description: Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, waka go meet the National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Nentawe Yilwatda, for him Abuja residence on Tuesday. This kain...

item.title: Historical Overview of Alcohol Served to French Schoolchildren Before 1956
item.description: Until 1956, it was common in France for children to be served wine, beer, or cider during school lunch. These beverages were considered nutritional staples, believed to aid digestion, provide energy, ...

item.title: Labour Suggests Reducing Employee National Insurance to Appeal to Voters
item.description: The news article reports on a Labour Party proposal aimed at regaining voter support by reducing employee National Insurance contributions. According to the party's recent report, the policy intends t...

item.title: How the 2026 Federal Budget Affects Different Generations in Australia
item.description: The 2026 Australian federal budget, unveiled by Treasurer Jim Chalmers, is being promoted as a step towards intergenerational fairness, but its impact varies widely across age groups. Gen Z, born betw...

item.title: Robert E. Cornish and His Experimental Resuscitation Research in the 1930s
item.description: Robert Edwin Cornish, born in 1903 in California, was a prodigious scientist who, by the early 1930s, became fascinated with reversing clinical death. He believed that if circulation and oxygenation w...

item.title: Los Angeles Chief Heat Officer Position Vacant Amid Leadership Changes
item.description: Los Angeles recently terminated Marta Segura, the city’s first Chief Heat Officer, leaving the Climate Emergency Mobilization Office with only one remaining employee. Segura, who took the role in 2022...

item.title: Mary Astor's Early Acting Career and Iconic 1920s Portraits
item.description: Mary Astor, born Lucile Vasconcellos Langhanke in 1906, was an American actress who began her career as a teenager. Her family supported her ambitions, allowing her to take on small roles in films suc...

item.title: Debts and housing top concerns for young Australians as budget reforms loom
item.description: As Labor prepares to unveil its latest budget, concerns over housing affordability and government debt weigh heavily on young Australians. Opposition figures, including Claire Chandler and Tim Wilson,...

item.title: Musician Xav Trudeau Scheduled to Perform at Ottawa Blackjacks’ Season Opener
item.description: Canadian musician Xav Trudeau is set to perform at the Ottawa Blackjacks’ home opener, marking a high-profile addition to the team’s 2026 season launch. The performance is expected to draw local fans ...

item.title: María Corda: A Silent Film Star's Journey from Europe to Hollywood

item.title: Solène Lory’s Memorable Stage Moment with Michael Jackson in Munich, 1997
item.description: Solène Lory, a devoted French fan of Michael Jackson, experienced a defining moment of her life during the HIStory World Tour. At 18, she was brought on stage during the Munich concert on July 6, 1997...

item.title: Kristi Noem vacates Coast Guard residence previously occupied by four-star admiral
item.description: Former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has finally left a waterfront Coast Guard residence that previously housed Adm. Linda Fagan, the first woman to lead the U.S. Coast Guard. Fagan was repo...

item.title: Raquel Torres: Pioneering Latina Star of Early Hollywood
item.description: Raquel Torres (1908–1987) was a Mexican-American actress who became one of the most memorable faces of late silent films and early talkies in Hollywood. She rose to fame at just 19 years old with her ...

item.title: Indian Rupee Declines to 95.6 Against US Dollar Amid Rising Oil Prices and Market Volatility
item.description: The Indian financial markets faced significant turbulence on Tuesday as the Sensex fell over 1,450 points, marking a decline of 1.9%, and the Nifty dropped more than 430 points, or 1.8%. Investors suf...
